_comment: RF Layer 1 training configuration — generated by dataset validation audit

rf_training_features._comment: Use these columns as X when training the Random Forest classifier

exclude_with_reason.SECCOMP_UNCONFINED: Zero variance (0/2048 = 0.0%) — provides no information to the model
exclude_with_reason.VALID_TAINT_SECRET: Zero variance (0/2048 = 0.0%) — complex taint analysis not implemented in extractor
exclude_with_reason.NO_NETWORK_POLICY: Near-constant (2047/2048 = 99.95%) — zero discriminative power for classification
exclude_with_reason.risk_score: Derived from features — would cause target leakage if used as input
exclude_with_reason.severity_class: Derived label — do not use as input feature
exclude_with_reason.label: Target variable

rf_target_variable._note: For binary: label=0 (secure), label=1 (misconfigured). For multiclass: 0=clean, 1=low_medium, 2=high_critical

near_zero_variance_features.DOCKERSOCK_PATH: 0.3% (7/2048) — low but retainable; RF handles rare features via tree splits
near_zero_variance_features.ALLOW_PRIVI: 0.2% (4/2048) — consider dropping if RF feature importance is near zero

high_feature_correlation.CAP_SYS_ADMIN_vs_CAP_SYS_MODULE: phi=0.816 — use cap_misuse (derived) instead of both
high_feature_correlation.NO_SECU_CONTEXT_vs_NO_RESO: phi=0.774 — co-occur frequently in minimally configured pods

sampling_bias.description: Top 10 repos = 60.8% of dataset. Use cluster_weight in RF sample_weight to correct.

checkov_extended_features._comment: These columns are filled by scan_with_tools.py for rows with downloaded YAMLs (has_yaml=1). Rows with has_yaml=0 have empty string — impute with column median before training.

gap_fills.checkov_no_run_as_nonroot: Fills SECCOMP_UNCONFINED gap via CKV_K8S_30 / CKV_K8S_40

checkov_extended_features.umi_equivalent: checkov_umi_score = checkov_failed / (checkov_failed + checkov_passed). GenKubeSec UMI proxy.

cluster_ids_for_gnn._comment: Each unique repo_name maps to one cluster snapshot for GNN graph construction. 82 unique repos in current dataset.

gnn_graph_layer._comment: Layer 2 graph dataset — generated by graph_builder.py from rf_dataset.csv + downloaded YAMLs
gnn_graph_layer.node_features: indices 0-17: 18 Rahman flags | 18-23: 6 extended features | 24: risk_score

edge_types.0: directory_proximity — manifests in same YAML subdirectory (undirected)
edge_types.1: privilege_reach — escape-capable pod directed to all others
edge_types.2: sa_lateral — SA-exposed pod directed to co-located pods
edge_types.3: semantic_namespace — same K8s namespace (from parsed YAML)

attack_chain_definition.label_0: All nodes have label=0 (no misconfigured resources)
attack_chain_definition.label_1: Has misconfigured nodes but no compounding chain (either 0 or 1 escape node, no escape→lateral path)
attack_chain_definition.label_2: ≥2 escape-capable nodes OR directed path from escape node to lateral-movement node

gnn_model.architecture: GAT (Graph Attention Network) with global mean+max pooling
gnn_model.recommended_eval: 5-fold cross-validation, report macro-F1 (class imbalance: only 13 chain graphs)
known_limitations: 82 graphs total is small for graph-level GNN training — use k-fold CV
badpods_hostpath labeled as 1 (isolated) due to missing hostPath volume flag in feature set
Flat-directory repos create dense proximity graphs (e.g. microservices-applications: 102 nodes, 10K edges)

imputation_strategy._comment: For rows where extended features are empty (has_yaml=0), use these strategies before training
imputation_strategy.NO_RUN_AS_NON_ROOT: median (expect ~1 — most real workloads don't enforce runAsNonRoot)
imputation_strategy.SA_AUTOMOUNT_TOKEN: 1 (conservative — default K8s behavior is to automount)
